Injuries that temporarily prevent a worker from performing their job, but from which they are expected to recover. This category can include incidents that result in days away from work.
Please enter the number of cases of temporary incapacity, due to work injury, for the given year:

Under Section 10 of the Hong Kong Employees’ Compensation Ordinance (Cap. 282), employers must track and compensate employees for days lost due to temporary incapacity, calculated as four-fifths of the difference between pre- and post-injury monthly earnings.
Please enter the number of lost workdays, due to temporary disablement cases, for the given year.

First aid is defined as the immediate assistance provided to a person who has been injured or suddenly taken ill, intended to preserve life, prevent the condition from worsening, and promote recovery. This includes both self-help measures and home care when professional medical assistance is either unavailable or delayed. First aid knowledge is considered vital, as it can be the difference between life and death in emergencies, helping individuals respond effectively to various incidents such as bleeding, burns, or seizures.\n
Please enter the number of first aid cases for the given year:

Dangerous occurrence per Schedule 1 of Section 14 of the Occupational Safety and Health Ordinance, Chapter 509. Examples include the collapse or failure of a lifting appliance, partial collapse of a roof, wall or floor, overturning of, or a collision with any object by a bulldozer, dumper, excavator or an explosion or fire that causes damage to the structure of any workplace etc.
